content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problem.text:
Let \(R\) be an integral domain, let \(P \subset R\) be a prime ideal, and let \(F=\operatorname{Frac}(R)\) be the field of fractions of \(R\). Define
\[
R_P=\left\{\frac{a}{b}:a,b\in R,\ b\notin P\right\}.
\]

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(R_P\) is a subring of \(F\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(R_P\) has a unique maximal ideal.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\omega\) is a root of the polynomial \(f(X)=X^2+X+1\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that the set \(\mathbb{Z}[\omega]=\{a+b\omega:a,b\in\mathbb{Z}\}\) is a subring of \(\mathbb{C}\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\mathbb{Z}[\omega]\) is a Euclidean domain with respect to the norm \(N(\alpha)=\alpha\overline{\alpha}\), where \(\overline{\alpha}\) denotes the complex conjugate of \(\alpha\in\mathbb{Z}[\omega]\).

problem.text:
Let \(R\) be an integral domain and let \(P\subset R\) be a prime ideal. Let
\[
f(X)=X^n+a_{n-1}X^{n-1}+\cdots+a_1X+a_0
\]
be a monic polynomial of degree \(n\geq 1\) with coefficients in \(R\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove Eisenstein’s criterion: If \(a_i\in P\) for \(0\leq i\leq n-1\) and \(a_0\notin P^2\), then \(f(X)\) is irreducible in \(R[X]\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of \(f(X)\) as above such that \(a_i\in P\) for \(0\leq i\leq n-1\) but \(f(X)\) is not irreducible.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(R\) be an integral domain and let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module. Define the rank of \(M\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\mathbb{Q}\) has rank 1 as a \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\mathbb{Q}\) is not a free \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module.

problem.text: Find a representative for each similarity class of noninvertible \(3\times 3\) matrices with entries in the field \(\mathbb{F}_2\) with 2 elements.
Let \(E/F\) be a field extension and let \(R\) be a subring of \(E\) which contains \(F\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(E/F\) is an algebraic extension then \(R\) is a field.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example where \(E/F\) is not an algebraic extension and \(R\) is not a field.
